system.docHtml:
Ophidiophobia is an intense, irrational fear of snakes. People with ophidiophobia may experience a range of symptoms when they think about, see, or are near snakes.

Impulse

Avoiding areas where snakes might be found, such as forests, deserts, or certain exhibits in zoos. Abberance (ABE) increases by 1 from the source of fear.

Disorder

In addition to trait intensity behaviors: Overwhelming fear or panic at the sight or thought of snakes. Abberance (ABE) increases by 3 (instead of 1) from the source of fear.

Physical Symptoms

Rapid heartbeat, sweating, trembling, shortness of breath, nausea, or dizziness.

Intrusive Thoughts

Persistent and distressing thoughts about being bitten or killed by snakes.

Emotional Distress

Deep feelings of dread and helplessness when near areas where snakes might be present.
